title: Detect New Fields and Log as 'New Field' in Change History
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to see when new fields are added to my Salesforce objects in the change history, so that I can track schema evolution over time.
description:
Extend the field change history to detect newly added fields during object refresh. A new "Change Type" column distinguishes between "Updated Field" and "New Field" entries in the history log.

### Workflow
1. User adds an object from Salesforce
2. A new field is added to that object in Salesforce
3. User refreshes the object in DataTools Pro
4. History log shows the new field with change type "New Field"
acceptance_criteria: New fields detected during refresh are logged in change history
Change Type column shows 'New Field' for newly detected fields
Change Type column shows 'Updated Field' for modified fields
ux_changes: New 'Change Type' column in field history table distinguishing 'New Field' from 'Updated Field'.
middleware_changes: Object refresh logic compares current fields with stored fields to detect new additions.
data_model_changes: New change_type column in the field history table.
implementation_notes: On refresh, new fields not in the local database are logged as 'New Field' in the change history.
comment_summary: Waqar implemented the feature and shared a screenshot of the new change type column.
